Hi there,
My name is

Emerson Kirby.

I am a software engineer based in Canada. I have many interests, but in the context of computing, I have been focused on app development (both web and mobile), health & wellness tech, and DSP.

I like to create things that people like to use.

You can reach me by and on LinkedIn & GitHub.

works   → kind words   → resume   →

Notes on working with me

A few notes from people on the teams I have worked closely with

"His expertise was super valuable with his out the box thinking and resourcefulness accelerating our technical progress."

"We had the pleasure working with Emerson on developing a medical device for incontinence management. Emerson brought both a strong technical and business lens to the development process. As a small start up, his expertise was super valuable with his out the box thinking and resourcefulness accelerating our technical progress. He was also a highly trusted source in helping us hire and lead technical staff. Would love the opportunity to work with Emerson again. Highly recommended."

"Emerson has a unique balance of technical expertise with the ability to communicate in a non-technical way."

"Emerson is highly collaborative, detail-oriented, and is always looking to enhance the user experience. He's an exceptional problem-solver and continually worked to optimize Lightheart's functionality and aesthetic. Emerson has a unique balance of technical expertise with the ability to communicate in a non-technical way. This allowed Emerson to translate our validated physiological data into code which significantly increased Lightheart's accuracy."

BONUS: Gobsmacked on the internet

"very cool idea, interesting take on how to reformat instructions so they're actually useful."

signal

"I write my recipe cards grouped like this as well. Beautiful!"

rebent

"Really neat tool! I've always grouped ingredients vertically and instructions horizontally, so I love how your tool automates this and makes it aesthetically pleasing."

kris-kay

"Love the name. The idea is intriguing. I can think of formatting some of my recipes for other people."

doctornemo

"This couldn't be more opaque to me."

tiny frying pan

"my gob remains unsmacked."

Greg_Ace
